target.title: fix(hooks): isolate slug-generator failures from shared auth profile (#71709)
target.body:
## Summary

### Problem

When the internal `generateSlugViaLLM` helper hits a transient HTTP 400 from the underlying provider (e.g. `LLM request rejected: You're out of extra usage` from `anthropic:claude-cli`), the failure gets recorded against the shared auth-profile as `disabledReason: "billing"` with `disabledUntil = now + auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` (default 5 hours). Every subsequent agent that resolves to the same profile then fails immediately with `Provider claude-cli has billing issue (skipping all models)` — for the full 5-hour window — even though the underlying subscription is healthy and a direct curl against the same key returns 200.

Reporters confirm the same shape repeatedly: a fire-and-forget slug-helper failure poisons profile health for every real user-facing agent on the profile. Workaround in the wild is `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.llmSlug: false`.

### Root Cause

`runEmbeddedAgent` accepts an `authProfileFailurePolicy` field with two values:

- `"shared"` (default): failure outcomes are written to shared auth-profile state and gate other sessions.
- `"local"`: `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` short-circuits to `null`, so this run's outcome never mutates shared profile health.

The seam exists exactly for low-priority internal helpers whose outcomes do not represent profile health, but `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` never opted in. It builds a `temp:slug-generator` session, fires one prompt, returns either a slug string or `null` — and the call site omits the policy field, so any failure inside the helper is treated as a real, user-facing, profile-wide reliability signal.

### Fix

Pass `authProfileFailurePolicy: "local"` from the slug-generator's `runEmbeddedAgent` call. The existing gate in `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` now drops any failure reason (billing / auth / rate_limit / overloaded / etc.) returned out of this helper, so the user-facing main agent on the same profile is no longer blocked by an unrelated background slug attempt.

### What changed

- `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts`: add `authProfileFailurePolicy: "local"` to the `runEmbeddedAgent` options, with a 2-line comment naming the contract and the bug.
- `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts`: one new assertion that the slug helper passes `policy: "local"` into the embedded run.

### What did NOT change

- Classification logic for billing / auth / rate_limit is untouched. Real user-facing billing 402s on a shared profile still record correctly and still drive the documented backoff.
- Other `runEmbeddedAgent` call sites are not modified. `agent-runner-memory` (memory flush), `commitments/runtime` (extraction), `commands/models/list.probe` (auth probe), `crestodian/assistant`, `cron/isolated-agent` and the main user-facing runners in `auto-reply/reply/` still default to `policy: "shared"`. Each is a separate decision — the probe deliberately wants to record health, and the user-facing runners legitimately represent profile health.
- No config schema, no doctor migration, no plugin SDK, no extensions barrel, no `docs/reference/config*`, no dependency changes.

## Reproduction

```ts
// Conceptual: production behavior on origin/main (pre-fix).
// 1. Enable `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.llmSlug: true` (default on).
// 2. Configure `anthropic:claude-cli` as the primary auth profile.
// 3. Let generateSlugViaLLM hit a 400 with body containing "out of extra usage".
// 4. Observe `auth-state.json` for the agent:
//    "anthropic:claude-cli": {
//      "disabledUntil": <now + 5h>,
//      "disabledReason": "billing",
//      ...
//    }
// 5. Send any prompt on any agent resolving to the same profile.
//    -> instant failure: "Provider claude-cli has billing issue (skipping all models)"
// 6. Direct curl to the same key against Anthropic returns 200.
```

Unit-level repro lives in `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts`. Before the prod change, the new test fails with `expected undefined to be 'local'`; after the change it passes alongside the three existing tests.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Slug-generator's internal HTTP 400 (e.g. `LLM request rejected: You're out of extra usage` from `anthropic:claude-cli`) was promoted to a profile-wide `disabledReason: "billing"` cooldown lasting `auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` (default 5h), blocking every user-facing agent that resolved to the same auth profile.

Real environment tested: Linux x64, Node 22.19, pnpm 11.2.2, vitest 4.1.7. Unit test against the production `generateSlugViaLLM` + the existing `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` policy gate. No live Anthropic key required; the policy gate already has dedicated coverage in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-profile-failure-policy.test.ts` proving that `policy: "local"` drops every recordable failure reason including `"billing"`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts`

Evidence after fix (vitest output): `Test Files  1 passed (1) | Tests  4 passed (4) | Duration  12.49s`. The new assertion `requireFirstRunOptions().authProfileFailurePolicy === "local"` passes, and bite was confirmed by temporarily reverting the prod line and observing the same test fail with `AssertionError: expected undefined to be 'local'`.

Observed result after fix: The slug-generator now opts into the lane-local failure policy. Any failure routed through `resolveAuthProfileFailureReason` from the slug helper returns `null` (no auth-profile mutation), so a transient 400 inside slug generation cannot register `disabledReason: "billing"` against the shared profile or trigger the 5-hour user-facing cooldown.

What was not tested: Live Anthropic 7-day-cap / 402 reproduction (no live provider key available in this environment). Sibling helpers (`agent-runner-memory`, `commitments/runtime`, `commands/models/list.probe`, `crestodian/assistant`) were intentionally not touched and are out of scope for this PR.

## Risk / Mitigation

The change opts the slug helper out of a shared-state write path. Risk surface:

- A real, persistent billing exhaustion on the user's provider key will no longer be detected by the slug-helper lane. **Mitigation**: the next user-facing main-agent call still runs with `policy: "shared"` (default) and still classifies and records the same condition correctly. The pre-fix behavior simply moved that detection forward by one fire-and-forget background event, at the cost of a 5-hour false-positive whenever that event was actually unrelated. Detection-at-point-of-use is the documented contract for the other already-lane-local paths (`format`, `server_error`, `empty_response`, transport-side `timeout`).
- This adds zero new code branches in the auth/cooldown layer itself; the seam (`authProfileFailurePolicy: "local"`) is shipped behavior already covered by `auth-profile-failure-policy.test.ts`.

## Change Type

fix

## Scope

- Internal helper opt-in; no public API, config schema, plugin SDK, or migration touched. `git diff --name-only origin/main HEAD` covers only `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` and `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts`.
- No dependency files touched (no `package.json` / `pnpm-lock.yaml` / `npm-shrinkwrap.json` / `pnpm-workspace.yaml` change). Dependency Guard not applicable.

## Linked Issue

Fixes #71709

commands.author: dredozubov

target.title: Fix isolated cron cold runner setup timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- let long isolated cron jobs wait up to 5 minutes for cold Codex runner setup
- keep the existing 60s setup watchdog floor for short jobs
- add regression coverage for the long-job watchdog window

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: isolated cron agent-turn setup no longer aborts at the old fixed 60s runner-start watchdog when the job has a longer timeout budget; the run stays alive past 60s and then reaches the new bounded setup timeout.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw cron runtime on PR head `2c969858b6389c7e3e056c1a5e51ab894868bc6b`, macOS, Node 26.0.0, isolated temp cron store, no external credentials or network services.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a real-time `node --import tsx --input-type=module` script from the PR checkout that instantiated `CronService`, created an isolated `agentTurn` cron job with `timeoutSeconds: 130`, started a manual run, withheld the runner-start signal, sampled state after 61s, then let cron reach the derived setup bound.

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the real-time cron runtime run:

```json
{
  "openclawHead": "2c969858b6389c7e3e056c1a5e51ab894868bc6b",
  "scenario": "isolated cron agent-turn waits before runner start",
  "configuredTimeoutSeconds": 130,
  "expectedPatchedSetupBoundMs": 65000,
  "after61s": {
    "elapsedMs": 61035,
    "runnerHookEnteredMs": 41,
    "abortObserved": false,
    "cleanupCalls": 0
  },
  "final": {
    "elapsedMs": 65067,
    "abortObservedAtMs": 65044,
    "cleanupCalls": 1,
    "cleanupTimeoutMs": 130000,
    "runResult": {
      "ok": true,
      "ran": true
    },
    "lastRunStatus": "error",
    "lastStatus": "error",
    "lastError": "cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start",
    "persistedJobs": 1
  },
  "events": [
    {
      "action": "started",
      "elapsedMs": 34,
      "jobId": "2ddaca0a-a12e-4b7c-b7d9-2ab0746ee121"
    },
    {
      "elapsedMs": 65061,
      "level": "warn",
      "message": "cron: job run returned error status",
      "payload": {
        "jobId": "2ddaca0a-a12e-4b7c-b7d9-2ab0746ee121",
        "jobName": "PR 86893 live setup-window proof",
        "error": "cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start",
        "diagnosticsSummary": "cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start"
      }
    },
    {
      "action": "finished",
      "elapsedMs": 65062,
      "jobId": "2ddaca0a-a12e-4b7c-b7d9-2ab0746ee121",
      "status": "error",
      "error": "cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start",
      "durationMs": 65014
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: at 61.035s cron had not aborted and cleanup had not run; at about 65.044s cron aborted the pre-runner setup phase and recorded `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`, matching the patched derived bound for a 130s isolated job instead of the previous fixed 60s abort.

What was not tested: a full external Codex provider cold start with live model credentials was not run; this proof isolates the cron watchdog path before runner start, which is the changed runtime behavior.
